title: /kernel
summary_text: `/kernel` did not activate. I failed closed and recorded a blocked bootstrap for run `kernel-bootstrap-20260402`. I launched the required Codex-side bootstrap lanes and wrote the receipt at [kernel-bootstrap-20260402.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/bootstrap-receipts/kernel-bootstrap-20260402.json), but the repo’s `/kernel` contract only allows activation when a valid three-voice shape is live. That did not happen here: - `kglm` failed live probe with `fetch failed`. - `kcopilot` failed with missing authentication. - `kgemini` only surfaced the env-selection notice and did not produce a bounded READY response. - `kcursor` was not ready per the specialist picker. Because neither `codex + glm + specialist` nor a valid degraded specialist-only shape was established, I did not emit the `Kernel orchestration is active...` acknowledgement. Runtime state reflects that in the ledger: [bootstrap receipt script](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh), [runtime ledger](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/bootstrap-receipts/kernel-bootstrap-20260402.json). If you want, I can take the next step in one of two directions: fix the bootstrap path so `/kernel` can succeed in this environment, or continue with a normal non-Kernel Codex flow for the actual task.
